qids.subject: Theory of Computation
qids.prose: Consider the following sets: S1: Set of all recursively enumerable languages over the alphabet S2: Set of all syntactically valid C programs S3: Set of all languages over the alphabet S4: Set of all non-regular languages over the alphabet Which of the above sets are uncountable? A. S1 and S2 B. S3 a

qids.subject: Theory of Computation
qids.prose: State whether the following statement are TRUE or FALSE. is recursive if both and its complement are accepted by Turing machines.

qids.subject: Theory of Computation
qids.prose: State whether the following statements are TRUE or FALSE: The problem as to whether a Turing machine accepts input is undecidable.

qids.subject: Theory of Computation
qids.prose: State the halting problem of the Turing machine.

qids.subject: Theory of Computation
qids.prose: Which of the following problems are undecidable? A. Membership problem in context-free languages. B. Whether a given context-free language is regular. C. Whether a finite state automation halts on all inputs. D. Membership problem for type languages.

qids.subject: Theory of Computation
qids.prose: It is undecidable whether: A. An arbitrary Turing machine halts after steps.
